title: Better-Than-Reference Purity (Hyper-Purity Risk)
source: synthetic (SP v5 Section 8.2.1 tricky scenario: hyper-purity may indicate altered immunogenicity or different impurity profile)
scenario_description: A biosimilar where SEC monomer is 99.5% vs reference 98.2%. The system must recognize that hyper-purity might indicate altered immunogenicity or a fundamentally different impurity profile. Better-than-reference is not automatically acceptable -- 'different = investigate'. The SEC HMW drop from 1.2% to 0.3% (0.9pp absolute within 5% purity tolerance) warrants investigation but not deferral since both values are low-level impurity measurements.
change_description: Biosimilar analytical similarity assessment. Biosimilar shows significantly higher purity (SEC monomer 99.5% vs reference 98.2%) and markedly lower HMW aggregates (0.3% vs 1.2%). While superficially 'better', this hyper-purity pattern may reflect a fundamentally different impurity/degradation profile with unknown immunogenicity implications.
